item.title: U.S. investigating Apache crash after first-ever sea drone rescue
item.description: The cause of an Apache helicopter crash near the Strait of Hormuz that led to the U.S. military's first-ever sea drone rescue is under investigation.

item.title: What will happen to home prices if mortgage rates stay high this year? Experts weigh in
item.description: Will elevated mortgage interest rates have an impact on home prices in 2026? Here's what some experts think.

item.title: Stellantis recalls more than 1 million Jeeps in U.S. it says could catch fire
item.description: Stellantis is recalling almost 1.08 million Jeep Wranglers and Gladiators in the U.S. that could catch fire even when they're parked and turned off, federal regulators say.

item.title: Trump says Iran deal could be signed in 2 or 3 days
item.description: Trump voices optimism for a "very good deal" with Iran in just a few days, as a tense truce between Israel and Iran appears to hold.

item.title: Bear that caused panic and school closures captured in Japan
item.description: The bear sightings had forced the closure of all 94 public primary and middle schools in a city just north of Tokyo.

item.title: Platner seeks Maine Senate nomination and more primaries to watch today
item.description: Maine Democrat Graham Platner is seeking to make the nomination official to take on Republican Sen. Susan Collins in November.

item.title: Pentagon adds Alibaba, others to list of sanctioned Chinese companies
item.description: The Pentagon has added several prominent Chinese businesses, including tech giant Alibaba, to its list of Chinese military companies, keeping them from getting U.S. defense contracts.

item.title: Drug suspects arrested after vehicle in high-speed chase hits pedestrians
item.description: One suspect drove a vehicle "at very high speed and recklessly, even hitting several local residents" before being stopped by authorities, officials said.

item.title: Teen charged over London arson attack on Jewish charity ambulances
item.description: 18-year-old Subhan Ahmed allegedly assisted with the torching of four ambulances used by a Jewish volunteer organization

item.title: Iran and Israel say attacks halted after Trump tells both to "stop 'shooting'"
item.description: Iran and Israel declare a halt to fighting as President Trump says both are seeking an "immediate ceasefire" after a major escalation in the 101-day war.

item.title: 2 pilots killed in plane crash en route to pick up former MLB star
item.description: MLB All-Star former catcher Yadier Molina said on social media that the plane was bound for Texas to pick him up, along with family and friends.

item.title: U.N. court chief prosecutor suspended over sexual misconduct allegations
item.description: Embattled International Criminal Court chief prosecutor Karim Khan was suspended after the court's oversight body referred him for disciplinary proceedings.

item.title: Raman tops Pratt in LA mayor contest; California governor's race unsettled
item.description: Los Angeles City Council member Nithya Raman surged past Spencer Pratt on Sunday in the race for LA mayor. The California governor's race remains undecided.

item.title: Wembanyama, Spurs hold off Knicks in Game 3 of NBA Finals
item.description: Victor Wembanyama had 32 points, eight rebounds and six assists in his first NBA Finals win, carrying the Spurs to a 115-111 victory over the Knicks in Game 3 on Monday night.

item.title: President Trump booed by MSG crowd prior to Game 3 of NBA Finals
item.description: Donald Trump was booed loudly by fans inside Madison Square Garden when he was shown on video screens during the national anthem prior to Game 3 of the NBA Finals on Monday.

item.title: Raman defeats Pratt to face Bass in LA mayoral election, CBS News projects
item.description: CBS News projects that incumbent Los Angeles Mayor Karen Bass will face off against Nithya Raman in November's runoff election.

item.title: Francesca Hong on her strong polling in Democratic primary for Wisconsin governor
item.description: Wisconsin state representative and self-described democratic socialist Francesca Hong launched what many would consider a long-shot candidacy for Wisconsin governor last year. But early polls show her as one of the frontrunners ahead of the Democratic primary on August 11. Hong joined CBS News to discuss her campaign.

item.title: Apple unveils new child safety features at annual conference
item.description: Apple unveiled new child safety features for its devices at its annual Worldwide Developers Conference in Cupertino, California, Monday. CBS News senior business and tech correspondent Jo Ling Kent has more.

item.title: Latest details on Trump effort to denaturalize 17 U.S. citizens accused of serious crimes
item.description: The Trump administration says it is moving forward with what it calls the largest-ever effort to use its power to revoke U.S. citizenship. CBS News immigration correspondent Camilo Montoya-Galvez has more.

item.title: 6/8: The Takeout with Major Garrett
item.description: Iran and Israel say they've halted military operations after weekend flare-up; Trump attends NBA Finals game at Madison Square Garden.

item.title: 6/8: CBS Evening News
item.description: Millions in central U.S. facing severe weather threats after weekend of storms; Inside Trump's visit to the NBA Finals.

item.title: Federal lawsuit aims to stop UFC fight at White House on Trump's birthday
item.description: A federal lawsuit filed Saturday looks to stop the UFC fight scheduled to take place on the White House South Lawn on President Trump's birthday this Sunday. CBS News legal reporter Katrina Kaufman has more.

item.title: Iran and Israel cease attacks after weekend hostilities
item.description: Tensions flared again in the Middle East over the weekend as Iran and Israel exchanged direct strikes. CBS News Charlie D'Agata and Nancy Cordes have the latest.

item.title: Political strategists react to Trump walking out of NBC interview, break down Wisconsin House race
item.description: Democratic strategist Joel Payne and Republican strategist Harrison Fields join CBS News to discuss some of the week's top political news.

item.title: Pulte appointment as acting DNI could hold up FISA reauthorization
item.description: Lawmakers on both sides of the aisle are pushing back against President Trump's pick of Bill Pulte to be the next acting director of national intelligence. CBS News congressional reporter Taurean Small has more.

item.title: U.S. bars entry of FIFA World Cup referee from Somalia
item.description: Federal immigration authorities barred a Somali soccer referee who was slated to officiate the FIFA World Cup from entering the U.S. over the weekend, citing "vetting concerns."

item.title: U.S. launches largest-ever effort to denaturalize citizens accused of fraud
item.description: The Trump administration on Monday announced it is seeking to revoke the citizenship of 17 U.S. citizens accused of immigration fraud.

item.title: Pope Leo denounces what he calls "scourge" of sexual abuse by Catholic clergy
item.description: Pope Leo XIV denounced the "scourge" of sexual violence by Catholic clergy and called for a "culture of care" in the Church ahead of an expected private meeting with victims in Spain.

item.title: Russian teen claims first Grand Slam tennis title with French Open win
item.description: The eighth-ranked Andreeva ended the run of 114th-ranked Polish qualifier Maja Chwalinska in the French Open final on Saturday.

item.title: Families, investigators remain haunted by unsolved Texas yogurt shop murders
item.description: Could new information lead to answers in the brutal murder of four teenage girls in Austin, Texas, more than 30 years ago?
